result.markdown:
--- title: "Curator Desk" format: html: toc: false --- # BioAnalyzer curator desk Use this page to review BioAnalyzer’s predicted statuses for candidate papers and record curator ground truth for each PMID. What you can do here: * **Find papers quickly:** Search, sort, and filter the table (Priority score helps surface high‑value candidates). * **Record ground truth:** Enter curator TRUE labels and optional “was the prediction correct?” flags per field. * **Share feedback:** Submit feedback via a GitHub issue (CSV is pre-filled in the issue body). ::: {.callout-note collapse="false"} ## Data Source & Setup The table is built from data stored in the **`data/`** folder. To analyze your own papers: 1. Place your CSV (or Parquet) file in `data/` (e.g., `data/sample.csv`). 2. Run `quarto render`. The system reads `data/sample.csv` by default. To use a different source, set the `CURATOR_DATA_PATH` environment variable to your specific path (e.g., `data/analyzed_papers.csv`) or use `CURATOR_DATA_URL` for remote files. **Expected columns** * **Required:** `PMID` * **Recommended:** `Title`, `Journal`, `Year`, `Summary` * **Status values:** `ABSENT` | `PARTIALLY_PRESENT` | `PRESENT` ::: --- ## Candidate Curatable Articles {.tabset #candidate-papers} ::: {.panel-tabset} ### Interactive Table **Tip:** Sort by **Priority** (descending) to review the most promising candidates first. Use the search box and column filters to narrow your focus. ::: ::: {.cell .column-screen} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset} **Rows in table:** 29 ## Curator feedback Use the table above to find papers (the **Open** button in the **Published** column opens the paper on PubMed). Then select or enter a PMID below; the page will scroll to the field-by-field validation form where you can enter your review. Feedback uses the same schema as the Streamlit app; because this site is static, **saving is done by downloading a CSV** per row—submit via your usual process (e.g. email or GitHub issue). 1. **Quick select** a PMID from the dropdown, or type a PMID and click **Load row** (the form will scroll into view). 2. For each field, review **BioAnalyzer predicted** and set **Curator TRUE label** and **Was BioAnalyzer correct?** 3. Click **Download feedback CSV** to save one row; submit as needed. ::: {#feedback-form .card .shadow-sm} ::: {.card-header .bg-light .py-3} **Curator feedback form** ::: ::: {.card-body} ::: {.mb-3} **Curator ID / initials** ::: ::: {.mb-3} **Quick select for feedback** — Select a PMID from the table, or type below. ::: ::: {.mb-3} **PMID**
::: ::: {.mb-3} **Title (from table)** ::: ::: {.mb-3} **Overall paper verdict** ::: ::: {.mb-3} **Comment (optional)** ::: ::: {.mb-3} **BioAnalyzer version** (fixed for all submissions) ::: ### Field-by-field validation (ground truth) {#field-validation-section} For each field, provide the **curator TRUE label** (ground truth). Optionally mark whether BioAnalyzer's predicted status was correct. ::: {.grid}
Host Species

Select a PMID above and click Load row to see BioAnalyzer’s prediction.

Curator TRUE label for Host Species: Was BioAnalyzer correct for Host Species?
Body Site

Select a PMID above and click Load row to see BioAnalyzer’s prediction.

Curator TRUE label for Body Site: Was BioAnalyzer correct for Body Site?
Condition

Select a PMID above and click Load row to see BioAnalyzer’s prediction.

Curator TRUE label for Condition: Was BioAnalyzer correct for Condition?
Sequencing Type

Select a PMID above and click Load row to see BioAnalyzer’s prediction.

Curator TRUE label for Sequencing Type: Was BioAnalyzer correct for Sequencing Type?
Sample Size

Select a PMID above and click Load row to see BioAnalyzer’s prediction.

Curator TRUE label for Sample Size: Was BioAnalyzer correct for Sample Size?
::: **Submit review** — Opens GitHub to create an issue with your feedback (no file download). A bot will then open a Pull Request with the feedback CSV so the maintainer can view and download it (including your initials and comments). ::: {.mt-2} ::: ::: {.mt-3 .pt-3 .border-top .small .text-muted} **Notes:** Submissions are processed as issues and a tab will be opened for you to just click the create button. Reviewers will be looking into you submittd issue to evaluate the results ::: ::: ::: ::: {#thank-you-modal .thank-you-overlay} ::: {.thank-you-card} **Thank you** for your time and input in helping to complete this review. Your feedback has been submitted; an issue and pull request are being created in the background. Would you like to continue reviewing other papers? ::: {.thank-you-actions .mt-3} ::: ::: :::
